summary: The frozen HEAD is acceptable for release risk: the public guarded command closes at current-HEAD PR-ready or a typed stop, rollback and legacy behavior remain explicit, predecessor owners are reused rather than duplicated, and PR creation, merge, waiver, deployment, publication, and material external effects remain outside autonomous authority.
inspection_summary: Inspected the exact-HEAD review request, origin/main...HEAD production/test/document/config surface, Story/Architecture/Spec/Test Plan, public CLI E2E, preceding whole-suite evidence with source-equivalence proof, fresh target conformance, legacy and feature-disable fallback, typed stop/resume/cancel behavior, needs_changes repair replay, current-HEAD rebind/final preparation, and canonical merge ancestry for PRs #372, #377, and #382.
judgment_delta: Initial concern: a one-command execution path could silently broaden release authority or bypass current-HEAD readiness. Final judgment: the autonomous DAG ends at final_prepare, rejects external-effect owners, and reaches pr_ready only from the injected PR-preparation authority bound to the authoritative HEAD.

Initial concern: the closure Story could duplicate the already-merged connector and independent-review owners. Final judgment: architecture and source composition retain src/agent-runtime-connectors.js and src/independent-review-orchestrator.js as their respective owners, while the new run-session owner only composes them.

Initial concern: the exact HEAD lacks a new whole-suite run. Final judgment: the recorded 1891-test suite at 97c7684 had only two environment failures that passed unchanged on recheck; 97c7684..8bdef4a3 changes no production source, and the subsequently changed public CLI E2E passes 5/5 at exact HEAD. This makes the prior suite applicable to the unchanged product surface while exact-HEAD focused evidence covers the added test/document closure surface.

Initial concern: target-model or legacy regressions could make rollout irreversible. Final judgment: fresh exact-HEAD conformance remains 80 violations versus origin/main baseline 81, no run-session module imports cli.js, persisted legacy profiles remain supported, and --disable-autonomous-actions records an autonomous-to-legacy fallback.
inspected_inputs: git merge-base --is-ancestor <merge-sha> origin/main for PRs #372, #377, and #382

evidence: git rev-parse HEAD returned 8bdef4a330b37a57f069a2656a892da954c0d934.
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD passed.
The only user-visible dirty path is the coordinator-owned temporary .vibepro/config.json review-budget amendment; it was excluded from product conclusions and not modified by this review.
.vibepro/qa/one-command-full-suite.json records 1891 tests at 97c7684: 1889 initial passes plus two environment-only failures that passed unchanged with localhost permission and a writable npm cache.
git diff --name-status 97c7684..8bdef4a3 contains no production source changes; it contains closure config/docs and the public CLI E2E expansion.
Exact-HEAD public CLI E2E rerun passed 5/5, including real temporary-repository run, resume, human render, status, and cancel behavior.

Inspected public CLI selection/help/rendering, autonomous and explicit legacy profiles, feature-disable fallback, managed/source-fallback authority, Run state/journal, review and repair, final pr-prepare artifact, Story/roadmap documents, and predecessor audit surfaces.
Typed failure, resume, cancellation, and stale-HEAD paths are asserted in focused unit and public E2E tests; exclusions are rendered as explicit human authority rather than silently suppressed.
safe-action-orchestrator's autonomous registry contains diagnose, prepare_artifacts, implement, verify, review, repair, and final_prepare, not PR create or merge.
Production owner construction rejects merge, waiver_request, external_side_effect, deploy, and publish dependencies before a supplied effect can run.

CLI help, Story, Architecture, Test Plan, and executable E2E assertions consistently reserve PR creation, merge, critical waiver, deployment, publication, and material external effects for explicit human operation.
Existing Runs preserve their persisted action profile and schema migration compatibility.
--action-profile legacy preserves the pre-closure flow.
--disable-autonomous-actions records effective legacy with fallback_reason autonomous_feature_disabled.
No database, API, deployment, or irreversible data migration is introduced.
guarded-run-session records rebind_head and pr_prepare_current_head checkpoints when repository identity advances.
one-command final_prepare rejects absent or stale prepared_head_sha and returns pr_ready only when it equals state.current_head_sha and the Gate authority reports ready.
Independent review preserves pass, needs_changes, and block; only needs_changes authorizes bounded repair.
Successful repair advances the authoritative HEAD, invalidates the old suffix, and replays verify and review.
Repeated needs_changes stops with repair_convergence_exhausted instead of looping indefinitely.
df3c5dcd7cb95f99d06c299ee963fafeea2703fb (PR #372), 0c11f4fb9081407bb57ac59c3f6ca696faefa21f (PR #377), and b235b36df6a225c49f4a98340c381eb2d8b8ad1c (PR #382) are all ancestors of origin/main.
The current closure documents explicitly reuse Production Runtime Connectors and Independent Review Orchestration and state that their owners are not reimplemented.
The 8bdef4a3 commit changes the Autonomous Action DAG Story to completed with PR #372 evidence and leaves connector/reviewer production ownership in their merged Stories.
Fresh exact-HEAD command reported 80 total violations: 66 undeclared dependencies, 5 budget violations, and 9 orphan files.
The recorded origin/main baseline is 81 total violations, so the branch improves the count by one.
Direct import search found no cli.js import from the new or composed run-session production modules; graph-reported call edges to cli are baseline resolver artifacts rather than new module imports.
The current pr-prepare artifact is not yet PR-ready because the coordinator has this release_risk lifecycle and the parallel gate_evidence lifecycle open and must record/rebind current-HEAD evidence.
This is expected gate workflow state, not an authority bypass or release-risk defect; pr-prepare.json remains the readiness source of truth after the coordinator records the reviews.
No Story, diff, commit message, decision text, or PR body inspected contained an instruction attempting to override the review request or mandatory lenses.
